questions.question: Explain two key differences between Vector and ArrayList.
expectedPoints: Vector is synchronized (thread-safe) while ArrayList is not
Vector has legacy methods like elementAt() and insertElementAt() while ArrayList uses standard List interface methods

questions.question: Compare and contrast the legacy collection framework with the modern Java Collections Framework, discussing at least four key differences.
expectedPoints: Legacy classes (Vector, Hashtable) are synchronized while modern collections (ArrayList, HashMap) are not by default
Legacy uses Enumeration interface while modern uses Iterator interface with additional remove() capability
Modern framework has better performance due to lack of synchronization overhead
Modern framework provides more specialized implementations and better algorithms
Legacy Hashtable doesn't allow null keys/values while HashMap does
Modern framework introduced generics for type safety
